judgment.summary: A well-featured div-grid Go board with solid interaction and controls but capture logic is absent, confirmed by stone counts not dropping when a surrounded group should be removed.
evidence_highlights: Turns alternate correctly and occupied-point rejection confirmed: zero pixel diff and move count held at 1 on second click to same intersection
Stone placement produces clear pixel changes each move; black stone at P21 reads brightness 18 (dark-stoneish)
Full control set present: PASS, UNDO LAST MOVE, NEW GAME, move counter, stone counts, game status
Center hoshi (P44) detected with 53-brightness contrast vs neighbours; only one hoshi visible
evidence_gaps: Capture failed: after 7-move sequence surrounding white at P11 on all sides, White Stones still reads 3 and pixel at P11 stays near white-stone brightness rather than returning to board color
Corner hoshi points (P22, P26, P62, P66) show zero darkness contrast — missing or not rendered
Stone-centering probe shows intersection is lighter than adjacent midpoints after stone placement, raising doubt about intersection-vs-cell rendering
No evidence of ko rule or suicide prevention tested

judgment.summary: Well-built canvas Go board with confirmed turn alternation, occupied-point rejection, and explicit suicide detection, but only the center tengen hoshi is visible and no direct stone-capture evidence was observed.
evidence_highlights: Status text toggles Black/White to play on every click with ~2000-pixel board diffs confirming stones land at intersections
Occupied-point click produces 'Illegal move' message with zero pixel diff, confirming rejection
Placing White into a corner surrounded by Black produces 'Illegal: suicide' message, proving liberty-counting logic exists
Pass, Undo, New Game buttons present; capture counters displayed; no external requests or console errors
evidence_gaps: Only the center tengen (P44) shows a dark hoshi dot; the four corner hoshi points (P22, P26, P62, P66) show no contrast against the board background
Capture scenario does not complete a capture — no stone removal or counter increment is observed, leaving actual capture behavior unconfirmed

judgment.summary: Well-executed canvas Go board with stones correctly placed on intersections, visible hoshi points, confirmed capture logic, occupied-point rejection, and a full control set; ko and suicide prevention are claimed but not independently exercised in the evidence.
evidence_highlights: Stone-centering probe: stone at P00 reads brightness 29 while midpoints read ~175 boardish, confirming stones render on intersections not cell centers
Hoshi contrast at P22, P26, P44, P62, P66 shows correct darkening at expected 9x9 hoshi positions, with center P44 at brightness 48 vs reference ~112

capture_probe confirms capture: white stone placed at P11 (brightness 205) later reads boardish (brightness 160, delta -45); pixel diff step 06→07 shows P10 switching from dark-stoneish (brightness 14) to boardish (164)
Occupied-point rejection confirmed: clicking P00 twice leaves second diff at 0 changed pixels and turn unchanged; all four controls (Pass, Undo, Reset, Score now) present with move history
evidence_gaps: Ko rule is asserted in the UI description but no test sequence exercises it
Suicide prevention is asserted in the UI description but no test sequence exercises it
Live area scoring shows anomalous 'Black 81 White 0' after the very first stone, which may confuse users even if it is technically consistent with area scoring on an empty board
